objections.description: Critical sequencing error: Step 5 (Standardize bottom-sheet patterns) occurs after Steps 3 and 4 which rely on bottom sheets for filters and TOC. This guarantees rework and inconsistent UX patterns during implementation.
objections.suggested_fix: Move Bottom Sheet standardization to Step 1 or 2. Define the primitive component before building features that depend on it.

objections.description: Plan lacks specific handling for dense legal text workflows. 'Section jumps' and 'TOC' are insufficient for DOU documents which require citation linking, text selection preservation, and virtualization for performance.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit requirements for citation parsing, deep-linking to specific clauses, and windowing/virtualization strategies for long documents.

objections.description: Visual direction relies on subjective terms ('warmer neutrals', 'editorial') without a concrete token mapping strategy. Developers will drift back to default shadcn dark theme without strict constraints.
objections.suggested_fix: Require a concrete token mapping table (CSS variables) in Step 1 before any shell refactoring begins.

suggestions.description: Define snap points and backdrop behavior explicitly in the design token phase to ensure consistency across Search Filters and Document TOC.
Implement a performance budget for document rendering (e.g., max DOM nodes) to prevent mobile jank on lengthy DOU entries.

diffs.hunks:
@@ -15,11 +15,11 @@ Execution order:
 1. Define design tokens and visual direction.
-2. Refactor the app shell, home, and search header into a more coherent product frame.
-3. Redesign results list and filter interactions.
-4. Redesign document viewer reading ergonomics and mobile controls.
-5. Standardize bottom-sheet patterns.
+2. Standardize bottom-sheet patterns (primitive components).
+3. Refactor the app shell, home, and search header into a more coherent product frame.
+4. Redesign results list and filter interactions.
+5. Redesign document viewer reading ergonomics and mobile controls.
 6. Revisit analytics only after real data backing exists.
